title: Starlink veterans launch startup to broaden megaconstellation ownership
summary: Former SpaceX engineers who worked on Starlink have launched a new startup to provide megaconstellation services to governments and companies seeking greater control over space infrastructure. The initiative aims to expand ownership and accessibility of satellite networks beyond existing providers.

title: ISS repair spacewalk highlights concerns about station health
summary: NASA astronauts are preparing for a spacewalk to repair a robotic arm on the ISS, while advisers express concerns about the station's long-term health and spacesuit conditions.

title: Europe’s next security challenge is in orbit
summary: Europe faces a security challenge in space, with satellite navigation underpinning over 10% of its GDP. The reliance on satellites highlights the strategic importance of space infrastructure for economic and security reasons.

title: Rocket Lab wins NASA award for three Electron launches
summary: NASA selected Rocket Lab to launch two science missions using three Electron rockets in 2027. This award highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting NASA's scientific exploration efforts.

title: Weather Stream releases first light imagery from GEMS2 microwave radiometer
summary: Weather Stream, a commercial weather satellite operator based in Boulder, Colorado, has released the first light imagery from its GEMS2-Amethyst satellite's microwave radiometer. The satellite was launched on March 30 aboard SpaceX's Transporter 16 rideshare mission.

title: Space Force’s new MUOS satellites to use Boeing’s 702MP spacecraft platform
summary: The U.S. Space Force will deploy new MUOS satellites using Boeing's 702MP spacecraft platform, which has previously supported commercial and military wideband missions.

title: Botswana signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Botswana has signed the Artemis Accords, joining other nations in adopting best practices for space exploration. This marks Botswana's formal commitment to international space cooperation.

title: Small satellite operators confront a bottleneck to space access
summary: Small satellite operators face challenges accessing space due to reliance on SpaceX's rideshare missions. The bottleneck highlights the need for alternative launch solutions.

title: Finding solutions to jamming and spoofing
summary: Iridium’s Tim Last discusses solutions to GPS jamming and spoofing challenges in the direct-to-device and IoT sectors. The conversation highlights competition and technological advancements in satellite communications.

title: House Appropriations Committee approves $55.5 billion for U.S. Space Force
summary: The U.S. House Appropriations Committee approved a $55.5 billion budget for the U.S. Space Force. The committee also emphasized the need for increased competition in satellite communications and criticized funding methods like reconciliation for the Golden Dome initiative.

title: Neon green auroras from space will take your breath away | Space photo of the day for June 26, 2026
summary: A stunning space photo captures neon green auroras from space, offering a breathtaking view unlike any other. This image is featured as Space photo of the day for June 26, 2026.

title: NASA's canceled Artemis hardware contracts reached $5.9 billion, audit finds
summary: NASA's canceled Artemis hardware contracts amounted to $5.9 billion, according to an audit by the NASA Office of Inspector General. The memo revealed significant cost overruns and delays before the contracts were terminated.

title: NASA is paying $30 million for a 1st-of-its-kind rescue mission to the aging Swift telescope before it falls from space. Is it worth it?
summary: NASA is funding a $30 million rescue mission for the aging Swift telescope to prevent it from falling out of orbit. The Swift Boost mission, launching June 27, aims to extend the observatory's nearly 22-year operational lifespan.

title: June's Strawberry Moon is unlike any other full moon. Here's why
summary: June's Strawberry Moon rises and sets at extreme points, tracing an unusually low path across the Northern Hemisphere sky. This makes it distinct from other full moons.

title: Watch live: Rocket Lab launching 'Ten Owl of Ten' mission for Synspective today
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch the 'Ten Owl of Ten' mission from New Zealand today (June 26) to expand Synspective's imaging constellation. The live event marks another step in commercial Earth observation satellite deployments.

title: Two 'super-puff' cotton candy exoplanets are the lightest gas giants ever discovered
summary: Astronomers discovered two 'super-puff' exoplanets with densities lower than cotton candy, orbiting the same star. These are the lightest gas giants ever observed.

title: This Prime Day, save nearly $700 on 'a camera of epic proportions'
summary: Amazon's Prime Day offers a significant discount on the Canon EOS R5 mirrorless camera, reducing its price by nearly $700. The deal is time-sensitive as Prime Day is nearing its end.

title: James Webb Space Telescope catches 6 galaxies merging into one of the largest galaxies in the universe
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has observed six galaxies merging into one of the largest galaxies in the universe, with a supermassive black hole forming at its center.

title: The Battle for Sullivan’s Island
summary: The Battle for Sullivan’s Island involved colonial forces repelling British troops on June 28, 1776, using the island's marshy terrain and inlet as natural defenses.

title: Expedition 73 Crew Reflects on Science, Teamwork, and Life in Orbit
summary: Astronauts and cosmonauts from Expedition 73 shared mission experiences and honored ground teams at Space Center Houston. The event included members from NASA’s SpaceX Crew-10, Soyuz MS-27, and NASA’s SpaceX Crew-11 missions.

title: NASA, US Small Business Administration to Announce Partnership
summary: NASA and the U.S. Small Business Administration will sign a memorandum of agreement to create a new interagency initiative supporting the growth of the American space industry. The agreement aligns with President Donald J. Trump’s National Space Policy.

title: NASA Welcomes Botswana as 68th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Botswana signed the Artemis Accords, becoming the 68th signatory and the sixth African nation to join the international agreement for responsible lunar and space exploration. The ceremony was hosted by NASA at its headquarters in Washington.

title: NASA Selects Rocket Lab to Launch Sun, Earth Science Missions
summary: NASA has chosen Rocket Lab to launch the PolSIR and TSIS-2 science missions under its VADR contract. The fixed-price launch services will be provided as part of NASA's dedicated and rideshare mission acquisitions.

title: Millions of Stars in Cigar Galaxy
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope observed the edge-on starburst galaxy Messier 82 (M82), providing new infrared data. The observations complement archival data from the Hubble Space Telescope for a more complete view.

title: A Turquoise Tint for the Black Sea
summary: A turquoise tint appeared in the Black Sea and nearby waters in spring and summer 2026 due to phytoplankton blooms. The phenomenon added a milky blue hue to the region's waters.

title: NASA’s TESS Mission Reveals the “Puffiest” Planets Ever Found
summary: NASA's TESS mission discovered two new 'super-puff' planets with densities comparable to cotton candy. These Jupiter-sized worlds are the puffiest planets ever identified.

title: NASA at the Ion: Orion Lessons from Artemis II Shape NASA’s Moon to Mars Path
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft recently completed Artemis II, returning four astronauts from a crewed journey around the Moon. The mission's insights are guiding NASA's plans for lunar surface returns and future Mars missions.

title: La NASA compartirá los últimos avances del programa Base Lunar
summary: NASA will share updates on its Lunar Base program, including a virtual discussion led by Administrator Jared Isaacman on June 30. The agency is advancing plans to build a lunar surface base.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. After 25 flights, the shuttle's new mission is educational.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the global launch manifest.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is preparing for repairs on the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy period of activity aboard the space station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing its Terran R heavy-lift rocket program, with the second stage now at Stennis Space Center, aiming for a 2026 debut. The company is progressing toward the rocket's first flight.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten space launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches are expected to take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider has not announced a new launch date yet.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. These developments follow the recent Flight 12 mission.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for a single internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. This marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of space exploration milestones.

title: The art of hadrons
summary: The news discusses 'The art of hadrons' without providing additional context or details. The term 'hadrons' refers to subatomic particles composed of quarks.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm developed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential impact on future missions. This innovation could streamline robotic operations in space.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a significant step forward for the mission's scientific objectives.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by ensuring spacecraft components are space-proofed.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: SpaceX alumni have collectively raised $9.2 billion for their new ventures, highlighting the company's role in fostering engineering talent and innovation. This trend underscores SpaceX's influence in shaping the broader space industry through spin-off startups.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered autonomous guidance systems provider. The acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's space operations capabilities.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to boost its space industry demand. This expansion aims to support emerging players in the launch sector.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission to address hacking threats and shared-compute modules on rideshare satellites. Their collaboration focuses on Space Fabric, SpaceComputer's solution for on-orbit security challenges.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan plans to select a proposal this month for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO. The initiative aims to enhance Japan's sovereign satellite communication capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Canada's Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association to represent NGSO satellite operators. The new trade association aims to advocate for the interests of non-geostationary orbit satellite operators.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a new era in the reentry sector. The event validates emerging startups in this growing industry.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches. This is highlighted in a report from NASA's inspector general.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space secured $7 million in funding and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demonstration flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit.

title: Ubotica Raises $11M for Satellite-Based AI Tech
summary: Ubotica, a Dublin-based space AI company, raised $11 million to advance its AI-powered intelligence platform for maritime security. The funding will accelerate the commercialization of its satellite-based technology.

title: Infleqtion Teams With Voyager in New Quantum Space Initiative
summary: Infleqtion and Voyager Technologies are collaborating with other entities to advance U.S. quantum space initiatives. The partnership includes Monarch Quantum, Armada, and the University of Colorado Boulder.

title: Pegasus Launch Set to Deploy Servicer Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus rocket is scheduled to launch a servicing spacecraft to reboost NASA’s Swift Observatory before it re-enters Earth’s atmosphere. The mission aims to extend the observatory's operational life.

title: Mitsubishi Electric Contracts MDA Space for Digital Payload for Japanese Defense Satellite
summary: MDA Space has secured a contract with Mitsubishi Electric Corporation to build the digital payload and antennas for a next-generation Japanese defense communications satellite. This collaboration highlights MDA Space's role in advancing Japan's defense satellite capabilities.

title: Capella Validates Mynaric Optical Terminal on Latest SAR Satellite
summary: Capella Space validated a Mynaric optical communications terminal on its latest synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite, marking the first deployment of an optical terminal by Capella. The company also released the first SAR images from the satellite.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct the satellite buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two satellites expected to become operational by 2029.

title: Space Force Awards Boeing $2 Billion MUOS Service Life Extension Contract
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Boeing a $2 billion contract to extend the service life of the Mobile User Objective System (MUOS) with two new satellites. The MUOS system, operational since 2019, provides secure communications for military forces.

title: Amazon Leo to Connect Construction Equipment for Hitachi Construction Machinery
summary: Amazon Leo has partnered with Hitachi Construction Machinery to connect construction equipment as part of its enterprise service preview. This collaboration expands Amazon Leo's reach into the construction industry.

title: The EU’s 2 GHz Plan: A Test Case for Sovereignty in Satcom
summary: The European Commission proposed re-authorizing the 2 GHz Mobile Satellite Services band beyond 2027, highlighting satellite connectivity's geopolitical significance. This move is seen as a test case for EU sovereignty in satellite communications.

title: LEO Operators Launch New Industry Group for NGSO Issues
summary: A new trade association called SpaceConnect Association was launched by Amazon, Iridium, Telesat, and Globalstar to represent Non-Geostationary (NGSO) satellite issues. This industry group aims to address challenges and advocate for the interests of NGSO satellite operators.

title: Spanish IoT Startup FOSSA Secures $10M in Funding
summary: Spanish IoT startup FOSSA has secured $10.5 million in funding to expand its sovereign satellite capabilities and international operations.
